qtype: graphing linear inequalities, identifying feasible region, optimizing linear expression within integer constraints
tex:

\newline
\[y \leq -12x + 6,  y \leq 3x - 4,  x + y \leq 5\]

(b) Find the largest value of $\,x + y\,$ in the region R, where x and y are integers.
\newline

content.content:

tex:
The table shows the number of people in different age groups at a cinema.
\newline
\begin{tabular}{|l|l|}
\hline
Age (years) & Number of people \\
\hline
15 ≤ y < 25 & 35 \\
25 ≤ y < 30 & 32 \\
30 ≤ y < 50 & 44 \\
50 ≤ y < 80 & 12 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

Dexter draws a histogram to show this information.
\newline
The height of the bar he draws for the group 15 ≤ y < 25 is 7 cm.
\newline
Calculate the height of each of the remaining bars.
\newline

25 ≤ y < 30  \\
30 ≤ y < 50  \\
50 ≤ y < 80

stem.content:

\begin{tabular}{|l|l|}
\hline
Age (years) & Number of people \\
\hline
15 ≤ y < 25 & 35 \\
25 ≤ y < 30 & 32 \\
30 ≤ y < 50 & 44 \\
50 ≤ y < 80 & 12 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

Dexter draws a histogram to show this information.
\newline
The height of the bar he draws for the group 15 ≤ y < 25 is 7 cm.
\newline

_rawBody:
The table shows the number of people in different age groups at a cinema.
\begin{tabular}{|l|l|}
\hline
Age (years) & Number of people \\
\hline
15 ≤ y < 25 & 35 \\
25 ≤ y < 30 & 32 \\
30 ≤ y < 50 & 44 \\
50 ≤ y < 80 & 12 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

Dexter draws a histogram to show this information.
The height of the bar he draws for the group 15 ≤ y < 25 is 7 cm.
Calculate the height of each of the remaining bars.

25 ≤ y < 30 \AnswerLine[][cm] \\
30 ≤ y < 50 \AnswerLine[][cm] \\
50 ≤ y < 80 \AnswerLine[][cm]
\Marks{3}
